segments.text: Hear this, you priests! Pay attention, house of Israel! Listen, members of the royal family!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Judgment belongs with you because you have been a snare set at Mizpah and a net spread out on Tabor.  You dug a deep trap in Shittim,  but I will punish you for all these things you have done.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I know Ephraim very well, and Israel cannot hide from me, for now Ephraim is a prostitute and Israel is defiled.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your actions stop you from coming back to your God for a spirit of prostitution is within you and you do not know the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Israel's pride speaks against them, right in the face. Israel and Ephraim will stumble because of their guilt, and Judah will stumble along with them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will go to seek the Lord with their herds and flocks,  but they will not find him, for he has given up on them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They have been unfaithful to the Lord and have had children that are not his.  Now the New Moon will destroy them along with their fields.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Blow the horn in Gibeah! Sound the trumpet in Ramah! Shout an alarm in Beth-aven! Lead the way, Benjamin!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Ephraim will be left desolate on the day of punishment. Among the tribes of Israel I will reveal what is the real truth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The rulers of Judah have become thieves like those who illegally move boundaries. I will pour out my anger like water upon them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The people of Ephraim are crushed, smashed to pieces in judgment because they were determined to follow human commands.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I am like a maggot to Ephraim, and like something rotten to the people of Judah.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When Ephraim saw how sick they were, and Judah saw how wounded they were, Ephraim turned to the great king of Assyria for help, but he could not heal them or cure their wounds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For I will be like a lion to Ephraim, like a fierce lion to the people of Judah. I will come and tear them to pieces, and carry them off, and no one will be able to rescue them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I will leave and return to where I came from until they acknowledge their wrongs, and in their distress they will seek my face and beg for my help.
segments.speaker: Unknown
